title: Floral Creamy Indulgent Egg Plant Stir-fry
description: A floral and creamy indulgent Filipino dish featuring egg plants cooked in a gluten-free friendly sauce.

ingredients.name: 1 cup sliced eggplant
ingredients.notes: Ensure they are small and thin slices to cook faster.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons butter
ingredients.notes: Organic if possible for better health benefits.

ingredients.name: 3 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Optional but adds a nice umami flavor.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup fresh rose petals (optional)
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with a pinch of rosewater for those not able to get fresh roses.

ingredients.name: 1 can (14 oz) coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Look for unsweetened variety for reduced sugar intake.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons cornstarch
ingredients.notes: For thickening the sauce, ensure it is gluten-free if needed.

instructions: Heat a pan over medium-high heat. Add the butter until melted and hot.
Add sliced eggplant to the pan, sprinkle minced garlic if using, and sauté for about 5 minutes or until they start to soften slightly.
Stir in coconut milk and bring to a simmer. Lower heat to maintain a gentle simmer and cook for an additional 10-12 minutes or until eggplant is fully cooked through.
In a small bowl, mix cornstarch with water to create a slurry to help thicken the sauce. Gradually stir into the pan mixture while continuously stirring to avoid lumps.
After the egg plant has been cooking for 10-12 minutes, if you have fresh rose petals or rosewater available, add them now and mix well.
Let it cook until slightly thickened and fragrant from the rose petals (if using). Taste and adjust salt and pepper as needed.
Remove from heat and stir in chopped cilantro just before serving for freshness. Serve hot with steamed rice or noodles if desired.
